practical_description: Entry-level ceramic used in riot and static defense. Cracks on first strong hit, unsuitable for sustained fire. Disposably reliable.
visual_description: Flat white tile with chalk texture, fracture lines bloom like dry riverbeds after impact.

practical_description: Blunt trauma tanking and cost-effective. Survives abuse but risks spall and ricochet. Needs coating to be safe.
visual_description: Dark grey, matte-finished slab. Pocks and pits visibly after hits, edges chip like dented tools.

practical_description: Modern rifle plate ceramic. Breaks clean under stress, minimizes secondary damage. Trusted baseline for military kits.
visual_description: Charcoal grey with smooth but gritty surface. Seams are polygonal; when cracked, it shatters like tough glass.

practical_description: Semi-metallic plate with high impact tolerance. Fails by compression and bending rather than cracking.
visual_description: Gunmetal shimmer, curved slightly outward. Surface has faint hammer-forged texture and heat-tempered edge glow.

practical_description: Premium military plate. Eats high-velocity rounds cleanly but crumbles with successive hits. Effective, not durable.
visual_description: Black monolithic tile, unnaturally light for its size. Feels dead to the touch. Craters into dust on failure.

practical_description: Composite edge-tech with high resilience. Redirects kinetic force. Excellent multi-hit retention. Rare and expensive.
visual_description: Carbon matte finish with micro-weave gloss under light. Seamless black skin over ceramic bone.

practical_description: Built for shrapnel and light hits. Fails by puncture and directional tear. Good for drones, scouts, or armor overlays.
visual_description: Flexible black mesh, shimmers like motor oil. Folds and creases when manipulated, then flattens itself again.

practical_description: Transparent rifle-stop glass. Strong but brittle after use. Used in riot masks and military optics.
visual_description: Slightly blue transparent slab. Smooth like tempered glass. Cracks in radial starburst under load.

practical_description: Extremely dense. Used in tank armor and heavy static mounts. Shatters incoming rounds but transmits a lot of force. Not wearable.
visual_description: Dull metallic plate with unnatural weight. Edges chip into flakes; dents glow under impact light.

practical_description: Exotic high-temp armor plate. Resists thermal fatigue and directional blasts. Hard to source, harder to shape.
visual_description: Off-white with grainy surface. Feels soft to the finger but rings under a tap. Smokes under sustained fire.
